name: JavaScript Constant-Key String Decoder
description: Statically decode constant-key XOR / charCode string obfuscation in JavaScript so a downstream scanner can read the hidden strings (C2 URLs, victim domains, timezone names, shell commands). Finds decode sites with regex/heuristics — a `String.fromCharCode(x ^ KEY)` / `x ^ KEY` applied over a STATIC encoded literal (int array, string, or `\xNN`/`\uNNNN` blob, incl. a `Buffer.from(p,"base64")`/`atob(p)` front transform) — resolves KEY when it is a small int literal OR a variable assigned a small int nearby (best-effort constant propagation), applies the XOR, and writes the recovered plaintext to <outdir>/decoded-strings.js for rescanning. READ-ONLY: reads bytes and does arithmetic; never parses as code or executes the input.

prerequisites.install_hint: Install Python 3.8+ — pure stdlib (re, base64, mmap), no other dependency.

safety.notes: Read-only static decode (regex heuristics + XOR arithmetic + base64/escape decoding). Never parses the file as code, imports it, runs a JS engine, or executes any part of it. Complements js-deobfuscate (which runs the decoder in a sandbox) with a zero-execution path for the constant-key case.

args.name: outdir
args.desc: if given, write recovered plaintext to <outdir>/decoded-strings.js for rescanning

args.name: --format
args.desc: json | text (default json — the machine result the RE provider consumes)

args.name: --max-bytes
args.desc: cap on bytes read from the input (default 64 MiB)

fields.decoded: array of {key:int, count:int, sample:string} (grouped by key)

payload.note: Zero-dependency static decoder. Two-phase: (1) detect decoder FUNCTIONS (constant-key XOR-charCode body, optional base64/atob front transform) and decode their call-site payloads, resolving payload/key VARIABLES by nearest-preceding constant assignment; (2) inline sites (arr.map(c=>c^K), s.split('').map(...charCodeAt^K), for-of over an array/string/escape-blob). Emits decoded plaintext to <outdir>/decoded-strings.js as JS string literals so the fold rescans it as DerivedSource. Custom obfuscation is heuristic — see SKILL.md for the shapes it does and does not handle.
